label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to find f(f(f(2^2025))), where f(n) is the sum of the digits of n. Hmm, that seems pretty big because 2^2025 is an enormous number. Let me think about how to approach this without calculating the actual number, which is obviously impossible by hand. First, I remember that the sum of digits function has some properties related to modulo 9. Specifically, the sum of the digits of a number is congruent to the number itself modulo 9.
